activities_projects.title: Critical Analysis of Periodisation
activities_projects.objective: Analyse different ways of dividing Indian history
steps: Write the periodisation proposed by James Mill
Write the modern division used by historians
Explain two problems with James Mill’s classification
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical understanding of historical periodisation

activities_projects.title: Colonial Perspective Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Understand how British historians interpreted Indian history
steps: Identify how British historians described Indian society before British rule
Write two examples from the chapter
Explain whether this interpretation is fair or biased
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical thinking about historical bias

activities_projects.title: Historical Sources Evaluation
steps: List different types of sources used by historians
Explain why official records may not always show the full picture
Suggest another source historians can use

activities_projects.title: Colonial Impact Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyse political, economic and social changes under British rule
steps: Write three changes introduced by the British administration
Explain how each change affected Indian society
Give one example of a long-term impact
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops deeper understanding of colonial influence

activities_projects.title: Historical Debate Activity
steps: Prepare arguments supporting British periodisation
Prepare arguments against it
Present your opinion in a short paragraph

activities_projects.title: Visual Source Interpretation
steps: Select an image or advertisement shown in the chapter
Describe what you see in the image
Explain what it reveals about society during that time
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns to analyse visual historical evidence

activities_projects.title: Administration Records Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of government documentation in history
steps: Explain why the British produced large numbers of records
Write two types of administrative documents
Explain how historians use them today

activities_projects.title: Colonial Terminology Research
steps: Write definitions of colonialism, periodisation and sources
Explain how each term is used in the chapter
Give one example related to Indian history

activities_projects.title: Alternative History Writing
activities_projects.objective: Encourage new perspectives on historical writing
steps: Write a short passage describing Indian history from the perspective of common people
Focus on their daily life and experiences
Explain why this perspective is important

activities_projects.title: Mini Historical Research Project
activities_projects.objective: Conduct simple historical research using different sources
steps: Choose a topic related to the chapter
Collect information from at least two sources
Write a short report summarising your findings
